title: scirpus interior
intro: Scirpus interior, also known as sweetgrass, is a perennial plant native to North America that belongs to the cattail family. Traditionally, it has been used in herbal medicine for its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antifungal properties, which can help to soothe skin irritations and wounds. The health benefits of sweetgrass include its ability to reduce stress and anxiety, improve respiratory issues, and even act as a natural insect repellent. Sweetgrass can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, or dried for use in smudging ceremonies, where it is burned to purify and cleanse the air and spirit.
